abstract: In this paper, we introduce the Adaptive Recursive Neural Network (AdaRNN) for target-dependent sentiment classification on Twitter. Unlike traditional models, AdaRNN adaptively propagates sentiment signals from individual words to a specific target by leveraging both contextual nuances and syntactic relationships. The architecture employs multiple composition functions, modeling the sentiment propagation process as a dynamic distribution over these functions to better capture complex linguistic patterns. Our experimental results demonstrate that AdaRNN significantly outperforms existing baseline methods. Additionally, we contribute to the research community by introducing a new, manually annotated dataset specifically designed for target-dependent sentiment analysis in the Twitter domain.
